title: Digital Clock & Countdown timer - Final Touch

content.problemStatement:
Perfect - we have completed our Project!

Here are some additional tasks that you can try out.


Can you create a display box around the `digital_clock`. We have created one such version - check out the code in the IDE
Currently - you cannot exit the  timer or the clock - it keeps going on for ever. 

Can you update the code to accept a user input to exit the `digital_clock` project?
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. For each test case:
- A line with an integer N (0 ≤ N ≤ 86400) – the initial time of the digital clock in seconds.
- A line with an integer Q (1 ≤ Q ≤ 1000) – the number of commands.
- Q following lines, each containing either the word "tick" (advance the clock by one second) or "exit" (terminate the current test case immediately). Commands after an "exit" for that test case, if any, are ignored.
content.outputFormat: For every "tick" command processed before an "exit" in a test case, output the current time of the clock in HH:MM:SS format on its own line. No output is produced for "exit" commands.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The clock starts at 5 seconds. Each "tick" decreases the time by one second and prints the new value. After five ticks the clock shows 00:00:00. The subsequent "exit" stops processing.
The clock is already at 00:00:00. "tick" commands cannot go below zero, so the displayed time remains 00:00:00 for both ticks. "exit" ends the test case.

First test case starts at 3661 seconds (01:01:01). Two ticks produce 01:01:00 and 01:00:59, then "exit" stops further processing; the last "tick" is ignored. Second test case starts at 0 seconds; one tick prints 00:00:00, then "exit" stops.

solution.approach: For each test case we keep a variable sec = N. We read the Q commands sequentially. If the command is "exit" we stop processing the remaining commands of this test case. If it is "tick" we decrement sec by one unless it is already zero, then we format sec as HH:MM:SS with leading zeros and output the string. The formatting is done by integer division and modulo operations. The algorithm is linear in the total number of commands.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of commands) = O(∑Q) ≤ O(T·1000)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output buffer
